title: davilla alata
intro: Davilla alata, also known as the climbing horsewood or wing-stemmed horsewood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Opiliaceae. It is a climber that is native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Philippines, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are known to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Davilla alata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy that has been used for generations in folk medicine.
